sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: ὦν αὐτὸς ἔσχε στέφανον εὐκλείας μέγαν ; 465
sections.translation: Shall I leave my station at the ships and the Atreidae to their own devices in order to go home across the Aegean ? And how shall I face my father Telamon , when I arrive ? How will he bear to look on me , when I stand before him stripped , without that supreme prize of valor

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι ταῦτα · πεῖρά τις ζητητέα 470
sections.translation: for which he himself won a great crown of fame ? No , I could not bear to do it ! But then shall I go against the bulwark of the Trojans , attacking alone in single combats and doing some valuable service , and finally die ? But , in so doing I might , I think , gladden the Atreidae .

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: τί γὰρ παρ ’ ἦμαρ ἡμέρα τέρπειν ἔχει 475
sections.translation: That must not happen . Some enterprise must be sought whereby I may prove to my aged father that in nature , at least , his son is not gutless . It is a stain upon a man to crave the full term of life , when he finds no variation from his ignominious troubles .

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: τὸν εὐγενῆ χρή · πάντ ’ ἀκήκοας λόγον . 480
sections.translation: What joy is there in day following day , now advancing us towards , now drawing us back from the verge of death ? I would not buy at any price the man who feels the glow of empty hopes .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: ὦ δἐσποτ ’ Αἴας , τῆς ἀναγκαίας τύχης 485
sections.translation: The options for a noble man are only two : either live with honor , or make a quick and honorable death . You have heard all .

sections.label: Section 481
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν οὐδὲν μεῖζον ἀθρώποις κακ ( ν .
sections.translation: No man shall say that you have spoken a bastard word , Ajax , or one not bred of your own heart . Yet at least pause ; dismiss these thoughts , and grant friends the power to rule your purpose .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original:
490
καὶ σῇ μάλιστα χειρί · τοιγαροῦν , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: Ajax , my lord , the fortune that humans are compelled to endure is their gravest evil . I was the daughter of a free-born father mighty in wealth , if any Phrygian was . Now I am a slave , for somehow the gods so ordained ,

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original:
495
τῶν σῶν ὑπ ’ ἐχθρῶν , χειρίαν ἀφεὶς τινί .
sections.translation: and even more so did your strong hand . Therefore , since I have come into your bed , I wish you well , and I do beg you , by the Zeus of our hearth , by your marriage-bed in which you coupled with me , do not condemn me to the cruel talk

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original:
500
καί τις πικρὸν πρόςφθεγμα δεσποτῶν ἐρεῖ ,
sections.translation: of your enemies , do not leave me to the hand of a stranger ! On whatever day you die and widow me by your death , on that same day , be sure , I shall also be seized forcibly by the Greeks and , with your son , shall obtain a slave ’ s portion .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original:
505
σοὶ δ ’ αἰσχρὰ τἄπη ταῦτα καὶ τῷ σῷ γένει .
sections.translation: Then one of my masters will name me bitterly , shooting me with taunts : See the concubine of Ajax , who was the mightiest man in the army . See what menial tasks she tends to , in place of such an enviable existence ! Such things will men say , and so will destiny afflict me

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original:
510
οἴκτειρε δ ’, ὦναξ , παῖδα τὸν σὸν , εἰ νέας
sections.translation: while the shame of these words will stain you and your family . Show respect to your father , whom you abandon in miserable old age , and respect your mother with her share of many years , who often prays to the gods that you may come home alive .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original:
515
πλὴν σοῦ · σὺ γάρ μοι πατρίδ ’ ᾔστωσας δορὶ ,
sections.translation:
Pity , too , my king , your son . Pity him the great sorrow which at your death you will bequeath both to him and to me , if robbed of nurturing care he must spend his days apart from you , an orphan tended by guardians who are neither family nor friends .
I have nothing left to which I can look ,

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: ὁ20 ἀλλ ’ ἴσχε κἀμοῦ μνῆστιν · ἀνδρί τοι χρεὼν
sections.translation: save you , and you are the reason . Your spear ravaged my country to nothingness , and another fate has brought down my mother and father , giving them a home in Hades in their death . What homeland , then , could I have without you ? What wealth ? My welfare is entirely in your hands .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: Αἴας , ἔχειν σ ’ ἂν οἶκτον , ὡς κἀγὼ , φρενὶ , 525
sections.translation: So remember me , too . A true man should cherish remembrance , if anywhere he takes some pleasure . It is kindness that always begets kindness . But whoever lets the memory of benefits seep from him , he can no longer be a noble man .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: κόμιζέ νύν μοι παῖδα τὸν ἐμὸν , ὡς ἴδω . 530
sections.translation: Ajax , I wish that pity touched your heart as it does mine . Then you would approve her words .

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: ἐν τοῖςδε τοῖς κακοῖσιν , ἢ τί μοι λέγεις ;
sections.translation: She will have approval as far as I am concerned , if only she takes heart and graciously does my bidding .

sections.label: Section 529
sections.original: Πρέπων γέ τἂν ἦν δαίμονος τοὐμοῦ τόδε .
sections.translation: Dear Ajax , I will obey you in everything .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: Ἀλλ ’ οὖν ἐγὼ ’ φύλαξα , τοῦτό γ ’ ἀρκέσαι . 535
sections.translation: Then bring me my son , so that I may see him .

sections.label: Section 531
sections.original: ἐπῄνεσ ’ ἔργον καὶ πρόνοιαν ἣν ἔθου .
sections.translation: But in my fear I released him from my keeping .

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: Τι δἦτ ’ ἂν ὡς ἐκ τῶνδ ’ ἂν ὠφελοῖμί σε ;
sections.translation: Because of these troubles of mine ? Or what do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 533
sections.original: Δός μοι προσειπεῖν αὐτὸν ἐμφανῆ τ ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: Yes , for fear that somehow the poor child would get in your way , and die .

sections.label: Section 534
sections.original: Καὶ μὴν πέλας γε προςπόλοις φυλάσσεται .
sections.translation: Yes , that would have been truly worthy of my destiny .

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: Τί δῆτα μέλλει μὴ οὐ παρουσίαν ἔχειν ; 540
sections.translation: Well , at least I took care to avert that disaster .

sections.label: Section 536
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , πατὴρ καλεῖ σε · δεῦρο προσπόλων
sections.translation: I approve of your action and of your foresight .

sections.label: Section 537
sections.original: ἄγ ’ αὐτὸν , ὅςπερ χερσὶν εὐθύνων κυρεῖς .
sections.translation: How , then , can I serve you , as things stand now ?

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: ἕρποντι φωνεῖς ἢ λελειμμένῳ λόγου ;
sections.translation: Let me speak to him and see him face to face .

sections.label: Section 539
sections.original: Καὶ δὴ κομίζει προςπόλων ὅδ ’ ὲγγύθεν .
sections.translation: Oh , yes — he is close by , watched by our servants .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original:
545
Αἶρ ’ αὐτὸν , αἶρε δεῦρο · ταρβήσει γὰρ οὒ ,
sections.translation: Then why is his presence delayed ?

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: νεοσφαγῆ που τόνδε προςλεύσσων φόνον ,
sections.translation: My child , your father calls you . Bring him here , servant , whichever of you is guiding his steps .

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ αὐτίκ ’ ὠμοῖς αὐτὸν ὲν νόμοις πατρὸς
sections.translation: Is the man coming ? Or has he missed your call ?

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: δεῖ πωλοδαμνεῖν κἀξομοιοῦσθαι φύσιν .
sections.translation: Here now one of the servants approaches with him .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original:
550
ὦ παῖ , γένοιο πατρὸς εὐτυχέστερος ,
sections.translation: Lift him ; lift him up here . Doubtless he will not shrink to look on this newly-shed blood , if he is indeed my true-born son and heir to his father ’ s manners . But he must at once be broken into his father ’ s harsh ways and moulded to the likeness of my nature .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: τὸ μὴ φρονεῖν γὰρ κάρτ ’ ἀνώδυνον κακὸν ,
sections.translation: Ah , son , may you prove luckier than your father , but in all else like him . Then you would not prove base . Yet even now I may well envy you on this account , that you have no perception of these evils about us . Yes , life is sweetest when one lacks sense , for lack of sensation is a painless evil

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: ψυχὴν ἀτάλλων , μητρὶ τῇδε χαρμονήν .
sections.translation: that is , until one learns to know joy or pain . But when you come to that knowledge , then you must be sure to prove among your father ’ s enemies of what mettle and of what lineage you are . Meanwhile feed on light breezes , and nurse your tender life for your mother ’ s joy .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: τηλωπὸς οἰχνεῖ , δυςμενῶν θήραν ἔχων .
sections.translation: There is no Greek — I know it for certain — who will do violence to you with hard outrages , even when you are without me . So trusty is the guard , Teucer himself , whom I will leave at your gates . He will not falter in his care for you , although now he walks a far path , busied with the hunt of enemies .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: Τελαμῶνι δείξει μητρί τ ’, Ἐριβοίᾳ λέγω ,
sections.translation: O my warriors , my seafaring comrades ! On you as on him , I lay this shared task of love : give my command to Teucer ! Let him take this child to my home and set him before the face of Telamon , and of my mother , Eriboea ,

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ αὐτό μοι σὺ , παῖ , λαβὼν , ἐπώνυμον ,
sections.translation: so that he may become the comfort of their age into eternity until they come to the deep hollows of the god below . And order him that no commissioners of games , nor he who is my destroyer , should make my arms a prize for the Greeks . No , you take this for my sake , Son , my broad shield from which you have your name .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: καὶ δῶμα πάκτου , μηδ ’ ἐπισκήνους γόους
sections.translation: Hold it and wield it by the sturdy thong , this sevenfold , spear-proof shield ! But the rest of my arms shall be my gravemates .

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original: θροεῖν ὲπῳδὰς πρὸς τομῶντι πήματι .
sections.translation: Come , take the child right away , shut tight the doors and make no laments before the house .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: οὐ γάρ μ ’ ἀρέσκει γλῶσσά σου τεθηγμένη .
sections.translation: God , what a weepy thing is woman . Quick , close the house ! It is not for a skilful doctor to moan incantations over a wound that craves the knife .

sections.label: Section 583
sections.original: Οἴ μ ’, ὡς ἀθυμῶ ! καί σε πρὸς τοῦ σοῦ τέκνου
sections.translation: I am afraid when I hear this eager haste . Your tongue ’ s sharp edge does not please me .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: Ἄγαν γε λυπεῖς · οὐ κάτοισθ ’, ἐγὼ θεοῖς
sections.translation: Ajax , my lord , what can you have in mind ?

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original:
590
ὡς οὐδὲν ἀρκεῖν εἴμ ’ ὀφειλέτης ἔτι ;
sections.translation: Do not keep asking me , do not keep questioning . Self-restraint is a virtue .

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: εὔφημα φώνει .
sections.translation: Ah , how I despair ! Now , by your child , by the gods , I implore you , do not betray us !

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: Ταρβῶ γὰρ , ὦναξ .
sections.translation: You annoy me too much . Do you not know

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν μαλάσσου .
sections.translation: that I no longer owe any service to the gods ?

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original:
595
εἰ τοὐμὸν ἦθος ἄρτι παιδεύειν νοεῖς .
sections.translation: Hush , no impiety !

sections.label: Section 592
sections.original: ὦ κλεινὰ Σαλαμὶς , σὺ μέν που
sections.translation: You will not listen ?

sections.label: Section 593
sections.original: ναίεις ἁλίπλαγκτος , εὐδαίμων ,
sections.translation: Yes , because I am afraid , my king !

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: πᾶσιν περίφαντος ἀεί ·
sections.translation: In the name of the gods , be softened !

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ ’ ὁ τλάμων παλαιὸς ἀφ ’ οὗ χρόνος 600
sections.translation: if you plan so late to begin schooling my temper .

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: Ἰδαίᾳ μίμνω
sections.translation: O famous Salamis , you , I know , have your happy seat among the waves that beat your shore , eternally conspicuous in the eyes of all men .

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original:
605
κακὰν ὲλπιδ ’ ἔχων ,
sections.translation: But I , miserable , have long been delayed here , still making my bed through countless months in the camp on the fields of Ida .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: θείᾳ μανίᾳ ξύναυλος ·
sections.translation: I am worn by time and with anxious expectation still of a journey to Hades the abhorred , the unseen .

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: φίλοις μέγα πένθος εὕρηται .
sections.translation: And now a new struggle awaits me , ah , me !— a match with

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: τὰ πρὶν δ ’ ἔργα χεροῖν
sections.translation: Ajax , hard to cure , sharing his tent with a madness of divine origin . It is he whom mighty in bold war you dispatched from you once far in the past . But now he is changed ; he grazes his thoughts in isolated places

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original:
625
λευκῷ δὲ γήρᾳ μάτηρ νιν ὅταν νοσοῦντα
sections.translation: and has been found a heavy sorrow for his friends . His hands ’ former achievements , deeds of prowess supreme ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original:
630
ἀλλ ’ ὀξυτόνους μὲν ᾠδὰς
sections.translation: have fallen without friends , without friends , before the unfriendly , miserable Atreidae .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: ἐν στέρνοισι πεσοῦνται
sections.translation: Surely his mother , companion of antiquity and

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ὃς ἐκ πατρῴας ἥκων γενεᾶς ἄριστος
sections.translation: grey with age , when she hears that he has been afflicted with the ruin of his mind will raise a loud cry of wailing . It is not the nightingale ’ s piteous lament
